title: Sorted Permutation Rank
hints: For each position i, count how many characters to the right are smaller than A[i].
For each smaller character found, there are factorial(n-i-1) permutations that would come before the current string.
Precompute factorials modulo 1000003 to avoid overflow.
The rank is 1-indexed, so start with rank = 1.
For position i, add count_smaller * factorial(n-i-1) to the rank.
Use modular arithmetic: (a + b) % MOD = ((a % MOD) + (b % MOD)) % MOD.
